title: Andrej Karpathy -- Let's build GPT from scratch
description: Watch and code along with Karpathy's foundational video on building GPT from scratch using raw PyTorch. The single best starting point for understanding training loops.

title: Andrej Karpathy -- Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M)
description: Deep dive into real training details: reproducing GPT-2 124M from scratch. Covers tokenization, data loading, training loop optimization, and evaluation.

title: Andrej Karpathy -- Intro to Large Language Models
description: High-level context on LLMs: what they are, how they work, and where the field is going. Great for building mental models.

title: 3Blue1Brown -- Neural Networks series
description: Backpropagation refresher. Visual intuition for how neural networks learn, gradient descent, and chain rule.

title: Umar Jamil -- Attention Is All You Need (from scratch)
description: Detailed transformer implementation from scratch. Covers multi-head attention, positional encoding, encoder-decoder architecture.

title: Jay Alammar -- The Illustrated Transformer
description: Visual walkthrough of the Transformer architecture. Excellent for building intuition about attention, keys, queries, and values.

title: Jay Alammar -- The Illustrated GPT-2
description: Visual guide to GPT-2 architecture. Covers decoder-only transformers, self-attention patterns, and generation.

title: Lilian Weng -- The Transformer Family v2.0
description: Comprehensive survey of transformer variants and improvements. Covers efficient attention, sparse transformers, and architectural innovations.

title: Lilian Weng -- Thinking About High-Quality Human Data
description: Understanding the role of data quality in training. Key concepts for RLHF and alignment work later.

title: Train GPT-2 (124M) from scratch in raw PyTorch
description: Train a small GPT-2 from scratch using raw PyTorch -- no HuggingFace .train(). Write the full training loop yourself: forward pass, loss computation, backprop, optimizer step.

title: Understand every line of the training loop
description: Go line-by-line through your training loop. Annotate: loss computation, backprop, optimizer.step(), gradient accumulation, LR scheduling. Be able to explain each.

title: Log training metrics to wandb or tensorboard
description: Set up logging for training loss, learning rate, and throughput (tokens/sec) to wandb or tensorboard. Visualize the training curves.

title: Implement checkpoint save and resume
description: Save and resume from a checkpoint manually. Save model state dict, optimizer state, scheduler state, and training step. Verify resume produces identical results.

title: Phase 0 Gate Check: Foundation Reset
description: Test your understanding of training loops, transformers, attention mechanisms, and backpropagation fundamentals.

title: Torchtitan -- Large-Scale LLM Training Using Native PyTorch 3D Parallelism
description: Modern systems deep dive into training LLMs at scale with native PyTorch. Covers 3D parallelism, distributed checkpointing, float8, torch.compile, and large-cluster training tradeoffs.

title: CUDA Programming Crash Course -- CoffeeBeforeArch
description: First 5-6 videos of CUDA programming basics. Understand GPU threads, blocks, grids, memory hierarchy, and kernel launches.

title: Mixed Precision Training -- Explanation and PyTorch Implementation from Scratch
description: Clear walkthrough of FP32, FP16, and BF16 plus why mixed precision improves memory use and throughput. Includes both the intuition and the practical PyTorch implementation.

title: PyTorch -- Profiling and Optimizing Applications with the PyTorch Profiler
description: Hands-on profiler walkthrough from the PyTorch ecosystem. Learn to generate traces, inspect GPU activity, and diagnose common training bottlenecks.

title: PyTorch -- Torch.Compile for Autograd, DDP and FSDP
description: Concise look at how torch.compile interacts with distributed training. Covers compiled autograd, graph breaks, and why DDP/FSDP performance changes under compilation.

title: PyTorch -- Introduction to torch.profiler
description: Official PyTorch tutorial on using torch.profiler to analyze training performance. Learn to generate and read profiling traces.

title: PyTorch -- Automatic Mixed Precision
description: Official recipe for AMP in PyTorch. Covers torch.cuda.amp, GradScaler, and autocast usage patterns.

title: Hugging Face -- Efficient Training on a Single GPU
description: Practical guide to maximizing single-GPU training efficiency: gradient accumulation, mixed precision, gradient checkpointing.

title: Stas Bekman -- ML Engineering Book: GPU chapter
description: Deep dive into GPU memory, compute utilization, and performance optimization from the ML Engineering book.

title: Lilian Weng -- Large Transformer Model Training Tricks
description: Comprehensive survey of training optimization techniques: parallelism strategies, memory optimization, and throughput improvements.

title: Train 125M GPT with mixed precision (BF16)
description: Train your GPT-2 125M model with BF16 mixed precision. Measure memory savings vs FP32 and record the delta.

title: Add gradient accumulation and verify loss
description: Add gradient accumulation (effective batch size 256, micro-batch 8). Verify loss matches non-accumulated training to confirm correct implementation.

title: Add gradient checkpointing and measure tradeoffs
description: Enable gradient checkpointing. Measure memory reduction vs speed tradeoff. Document the memory saved and additional compute cost.

title: Profile training with torch.profiler
description: Profile a training run with torch.profiler. Generate a Chrome trace. Identify data loading time, forward time, backward time, and optimizer time.

title: Use torch.cuda.memory_summary and try torch.compile
description: Analyze peak memory and allocation patterns with torch.cuda.memory_summary(). Then apply torch.compile to your training loop and measure speedup.

title: Create and identify a CPU bottleneck with profiler
description: Deliberately create a CPU bottleneck (slow data loader) and use the profiler to identify it. Practice reading traces to find non-GPU bottlenecks.

title: Phase 1 Gate Check: PyTorch Internals & GPU Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, profiling, GPU memory hierarchy, and training optimization.

title: PyTorch -- Getting Started with Distributed Data Parallel (DDP)
description: Official PyTorch DDP tutorial video. Covers all-reduce, NCCL backend, and torchrun launcher basics.

title: Yannic Kilcher -- ZeRO & DeepSpeed: Memory Efficient Training
description: Deep explanation of ZeRO optimization stages and how DeepSpeed enables training of models that don't fit in GPU memory.

title: IBM / PyTorch -- Lessons Learned Scaling Cloud-Native FSDP to 20B Parameters
description: Practical field report on scaling FSDP in production-like training systems. Covers communication bottlenecks, checkpointing, dataloaders, and stability lessons from 20B-parameter training.

title: PyTorch -- A Distributed Stateful Dataloader for Large-Scale Pretraining
description: Hidden-gem systems talk on the less glamorous but critical part of scaling training: the dataloader. Covers checkpointable, stateful, rescalable data loading for multi-node pretraining.

title: DeepSpeed -- Getting Started Guide and ZeRO Tutorial
description: Canonical DeepSpeed setup material. Covers installation, launcher usage, configuration, and the ZeRO memory-optimization path without relying on a weak placeholder video.

title: PyTorch -- DDP Tutorial (official)
description: Official PyTorch DDP tutorial. Covers DistributedDataParallel module wrapping, process group initialization, and gradient synchronization.

title: PyTorch -- FSDP Tutorial (official)
description: Official FSDP tutorial covering FullyShardedDataParallel wrapping, sharding strategies, and mixed precision setup.

title: Hugging Face -- Fully Sharded Data Parallel
description: Hugging Face guide on using FSDP with the Transformers library. Practical integration patterns.

title: DeepSpeed -- Getting Started Guide & ZeRO Stages
description: Official DeepSpeed documentation covering installation, getting started, and detailed ZeRO stage explanations.

title: Stas Bekman -- The Technology Behind BLOOM Training (blog)
description: Detailed blog post on how BLOOM 176B was trained using Megatron-DeepSpeed. Real-world distributed training at scale.

title: Lilian Weng -- How to Train Really Large Models on Many GPUs
description: Survey of parallelism strategies: data parallelism, tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism, and expert parallelism.

title: Convert single-GPU training to DDP with torchrun
description: Convert your single-GPU training script to use DistributedDataParallel. Use torchrun to launch. Understand all-reduce, NCCL backend, ring-allreduce algorithm.

title: Measure DDP scaling efficiency
description: Measure scaling efficiency on 2+ GPUs. Target: 1.8-1.9x speedup. Handle gradient synchronization, sampler distribution, rank-0 logging.

title: Convert DDP to FSDP with sharding strategies
description: Convert DDP code to FSDP. Experiment with FULL_SHARD, SHARD_GRAD_OP, NO_SHARD. Compare memory usage: DDP vs FSDP.

title: Integrate DeepSpeed ZeRO stages 1, 2, and 3
description: Integrate DeepSpeed with your training script. Train with ZeRO Stage 1 (optimizer state), Stage 2 (+gradients), and Stage 3 (+parameters). Compare FSDP vs ZeRO-3.

title: Study tensor & pipeline parallelism in torchtitan
description: Read Megatron-LM paper. Study how torchtitan implements tensor and pipeline parallelism. Understand splitting attention heads, MLP layers, micro-batching, and bubble overhead.

title: Build multi-GPU training script with distributed checkpointing
description: Build a complete multi-GPU training script: torchrun launcher, distributed checkpoint save/load, fault tolerance with auto-resume, rank-0 wandb logging, YAML config-driven.

title: Phase 2 Gate Check: Distributed Training
description: Test your understanding of DDP, FSDP, DeepSpeed ZeRO stages, distributed concepts, and checkpoint management.

title: Luis Serrano -- A Friendly Introduction to Deep Reinforcement Learning
description: RL intuition before RLHF. Covers MDPs, rewards, Bellman equations, Q-networks, and policy gradients in a learner-friendly way so alignment algorithms do not feel like magic.

title: Yannic Kilcher -- InstructGPT / RLHF Paper Explained
description: Paper walkthrough of InstructGPT (Ouyang et al., 2022). The original RLHF pipeline: SFT, reward model, PPO optimization.

title: Yannic Kilcher -- DPO: Direct Preference Optimization
description: Paper walkthrough of DPO (Rafailov et al., 2023). How DPO eliminates the reward model by directly optimizing on preference data.

title: Luis Serrano -- Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
description: Clear RLHF bridge for LLM learners. Covers how human preferences, PPO, and DPO fit into the post-training pipeline without overwhelming the learner with notation.

title: DeepSeek R1 Theory Tutorial -- Architecture, GRPO, KL Divergence
description: Strong modern explainer for reasoning-model RL. Covers DeepSeek-R1, GRPO, and the role of KL regularization in post-training.

title: Lilian Weng -- Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning
description: Nov 2024 post, highly relevant to Anthropic's work. Covers reward hacking, Goodhart's law in RLHF, and mitigation strategies.

title: Hugging Face -- Illustrating RLHF
description: Foundational visual guide to RLHF. Covers the three steps: SFT, reward model training, and RL optimization.

title: Hugging Face -- StackLLaMA: Train LLaMA with RLHF
description: Hands-on RLHF guide using TRL. Full pipeline walkthrough with code examples.

title: Hugging Face -- Fine-tune Llama 2 with DPO
description: Practical DPO tutorial using TRL DPOTrainer. Covers data formatting, training setup, and evaluation.

title: Hugging Face -- Putting RL back in RLHF (GRPO)
description: Jun 2024 post on GRPO and modern RL methods for alignment. Covers how TRL integrates GRPO for efficient training.

title: Hugging Face -- Open-R1: Open reproduction of DeepSeek-R1
description: Jan 2025 post on reproducing DeepSeek-R1. Cutting-edge RL for reasoning capabilities.

title: Anthropic -- Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant
description: Understand Anthropic's approach to alignment. Key paper for understanding the company's methodology.

title: Anthropic -- Constitutional AI
description: Anthropic's Constitutional AI paper. Understand RLAIF and self-improvement through principles.

title: Nathan Lambert / Interconnects -- RLHF series
description: Best independent RLHF commentary. Read the latest posts on PPO vs DPO vs GRPO tradeoffs.

title: SFT: Fine-tune Llama-3.1-8B with LoRA and full fine-tune
description: Fine-tune Llama-3.1-8B on UltraChat using TRL SFTTrainer. Do LoRA first (single GPU), then full fine-tune with FSDP. Compare quality on MT-Bench.

title: Train a reward model on Anthropic HH-RLHF
description: Train a reward model on Anthropic's HH-RLHF dataset using TRL RewardTrainer. Understand Bradley-Terry loss, margin loss. Evaluate accuracy on held-out preference pairs.

title: Run PPO with TRL PPOTrainer
description: Run PPO using TRL PPOTrainer with your SFT model + reward model. Manage 4 models in memory. Understand KL penalty, GAE, value function baseline.

title: Run DPO with TRL DPOTrainer
description: Run DPO using TRL DPOTrainer. Compare PPO vs DPO outputs on same prompts. Understand the implicit reward model in DPO.

title: Run GRPO with TRL GRPOTrainer + vLLM
description: Run GRPO using TRL GRPOTrainer with vLLM acceleration. Understand group sampling, relative advantage computation. This is what DeepSeek-R1 uses.

title: Distributed RLHF with OpenRLHF (Ray + vLLM + DeepSpeed)
description: Run the full PPO pipeline distributed across multiple GPUs using OpenRLHF. Understand how Ray orchestrates actor-critic-reference-reward across GPU workers.

title: Phase 3 Gate Check: RLHF & Alignment Algorithms
description: Test your understanding of SFT, reward models, PPO, DPO, GRPO, the RLHF pipeline, and alignment concepts.

title: PyCon -- Understanding the GIL
description: Deep dive into Python's Global Interpreter Lock. Understand why it matters for ML training data pipelines.

title: Sam Gross -- Multithreaded Python without the GIL
description: Canonical no-GIL talk from the engineer behind the effort. Explains what free-threaded Python changes and why it matters for Python-heavy systems workloads.

title: vLLM -- Easy, Fast, and Cheap LLM Serving for Everyone
description: Inference and serving deserves explicit coverage. This talk introduces vLLM, serving architecture, PagedAttention, and the real systems considerations behind high-throughput inference.

title: PyTorch -- torch.compile: The Missing Manual
description: Official torch.compile explainer that builds the right mental model for guards, recompilation, and performance tradeoffs instead of treating compile as a magic speed button.

title: Stas Bekman -- ML Engineering: Debugging, Performance, Fault Tolerance
description: Key chapters from the ML Engineering book on debugging training issues, optimizing performance, and building fault-tolerant systems.

title: Horace He -- Making Deep Learning Go Brrrr
description: Excellent intuition builder for GPU optimization. Covers compute vs memory bound operations, roofline model, and optimization strategies.

title: Tri Dao -- FlashAttention Blog
description: Understand memory-aware algorithms. FlashAttention: IO-aware exact attention that reduces memory from quadratic to linear.

title: GIL contention: profile and fix a multi-threaded data pipeline
description: Write a multi-threaded data preprocessing pipeline that hits GIL bottleneck. Profile with py-spy. Fix using multiprocessing, then try Python 3.13 free-threaded mode.

title: Master profiling tools: cProfile, py-spy, scalene, line_profiler
description: Practice with multiple Python profiling tools. Use cProfile for function-level timing, py-spy for sampling, scalene for CPU+GPU+memory, and line_profiler for line-by-line.

title: Memory profiling with tracemalloc and memray
description: Use tracemalloc and memray to find memory leaks in a training script. Understand allocation patterns and identify unexpected memory growth.

title: Data loading optimization benchmark
description: Benchmark data loader configs: num_workers (0, 2, 4, 8), pin_memory, persistent_workers, prefetch_factor. Find the optimal configuration for your setup.

title: Build training job launcher (CLI + YAML config)
description: Build a CLI tool: takes YAML config, validates it, launches distributed training via torchrun, monitors GPU utilization, collects logs, handles failures, supports auto-resume.

title: Build async checkpoint manager
description: Build a checkpoint manager: async non-blocking saves, retention policy (keep last N, every Nth), distributed/sharded checkpoint support, resume from any checkpoint.

title: Build training metrics collector
description: Build a lightweight monitor: tokens/sec throughput, GPU memory utilization over time, MFU (Model FLOPs Utilization), loss curves with smoothing, LR schedule visualization.

title: Phase 4 Gate Check: Systems Engineering & Performance
description: Test your understanding of GIL, profiling tools, systems engineering, checkpoint management, and performance optimization.

title: Andrew Ng / DeepLearning.AI -- Build Effective Agentic AI Systems
description: High-level entry point to agentic workflows from DeepLearning.AI. Covers planning, tool use, reflection, and why agent systems are more than prompt wrappers.

title: Krish Naik -- Getting Started with LangGraph for Stateful Multi-Agent Systems
description: Practical tutorial for building stateful agent workflows with LangGraph. Covers graph structure, state management, and multi-step orchestration with code.

title: Barry Zhang (Anthropic) -- How We Build Effective Agents
description: Concrete practitioner talk from an Anthropic engineer on how agent systems are actually built in practice. Pairs well with the official Anthropic essay for principles plus implementation nuance.

title: Anthropic -- Building Effective Agents (blog)
description: Official Anthropic guide on agent design. Covers patterns, pitfalls, and best practices.

title: Lilian Weng -- LLM Powered Autonomous Agents
description: Comprehensive survey of LLM-based agent architectures. Covers planning, memory, tool use, and multi-agent systems.

title: Hugging Face -- OpenEnv + TRL Integration
description: RL training for agents using environments. Directly connects agent work to training systems via TRL integration.

title: Fine-tune model for tool-use with SFT + DPO
description: Fine-tune a small model (Llama-3.1-8B) specifically for tool-use using SFT + DPO on tool-use preference data.

title: Set up agent training loop with environment interaction
description: Set up an agent training loop where the model interacts with a simple environment (calculator, file system), gets reward signals, and improves via GRPO using TRL + OpenEnv.

title: Study OpenRLHF multi-turn agent training
description: Study how OpenRLHF handles multi-turn agent training (the --agent flag). Understand training for multi-step conversations with tool use and RLHF for code agents.

title: Phase 5 Gate Check: Agentic AI & Training Systems
description: Test your understanding of agent training, tool-use RL, environment-based training, and the intersection of agent building and model training.

title: Set up GitHub portfolio repository
description: Create and structure the rlhf-engineering/ repo with README, training scripts, distributed configs, infrastructure tools, and benchmarks.

title: Submit OSS PR to trl, OpenRLHF, or torchtitan
description: Make a real PR to huggingface/trl, OpenRLHF/OpenRLHF, pytorch/torchtitan, or vllm-project/vllm. Even small PRs (doc fixes, bug reports, benchmarks) signal expertise.

title: Write blog post: Profiling RLHF Training
description: Write 'Profiling RLHF Training: Where the Time Goes' -- torch.profiler analysis of PPO training. Publish on Medium, personal site, or dev.to.

title: Write blog post: DDP vs FSDP vs DeepSpeed
description: Write 'DDP vs FSDP vs DeepSpeed: A Practical Comparison for LLM Training' -- your benchmark results and analysis.

title: Write blog post: From Building Agents to Training Them
description: Write 'From Building Agents to Training Them' -- unique angle combining Scania-Coworker agent experience with training systems knowledge.

title: Document benchmark results
description: Document scaling efficiency (DDP vs FSDP vs DeepSpeed), profiling analysis (where time goes in RLHF), and algorithm comparison (PPO vs DPO vs GRPO) in the portfolio repo.

title: Build agentic evaluation harness
description: Build evaluation harnesses for agent capabilities: tool selection accuracy, task completion rate. This feeds back into training signal design.

title: Phase 6 Gate Check: Portfolio & Open Source
description: Final assessment: portfolio quality, OSS contributions, resume positioning, and overall readiness.
